# Principles of Creative Learning

**Principle 1: Projects. Learning happens through making.**

**Principle 2: Passion. Interest drives effort and persistence.**

**Principle 3: Peers. Creativity is amplified by collaboration.**

**Principle 4: Play. Experimentation without fear of failure.**

**Case: The Game Designer. A child creates a space game. The spiral begins.**

**Case: The Clubhouse. Peers help each other solve problems and improve projects.**

**Case: The Scratch Community. Millions of children share and remix each other's work.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The Clubhouse Mentors. Adults support children's projects without taking over.**
**Case: The Remix. Scratch allows children to remix each other's projects. This is peer learning.**
**Case: The Exhibition. Clubhouse members share their projects in public exhibitions.**
**Case: The Passion Project. A child who loves animals makes a game about endangered species.**
**Case: The Mentor. A mentor who listens and asks questions supports better than one who directs.**
**Case: The Community. Scratch has a global community of young creators sharing work.**
**Case: The Diversity. Scratch projects range from games to stories to animations to simulations.**
**Case: The Choice. When children choose what to make, they invest more effort.**
